chapter_title.en: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations

text: 4278 - وَ سُئِلَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ غَضِبَ فَقَالَ عَلَيَّ اَلْمَشْيُ إِلَى بَيْتِ اَللَّهِ اَلْحَرَامِ قَالَ "إِذَا لَمْ يَقُلْ لِلَّهِ عَلَيَّ فَلَيْسَ بِشَيْءٍ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.4278 - Imam (as) was asked about a man who, in a state of anger, said:
"Upon me is to walk to the Sacred House of Allah (swt)."
Imam (as) said: "If he did not say, 'It is a vow to Allah (swt) upon me,' then it is of no consequence."
